summary: Enhancement 12 — AI Document Summarizer tested end-to-end. Backend: 15/15 pytest cases passed (focuses list, history list, summarize success with focus=financial/save=true via real Claude, invalid focus 400, no-file 422, empty 400, oversized 13MB 400, unsupported zip MIME 400, save=false does not persist, minimal PDF via reportlab summarizes 200, history list excludes extracted_text/content, GET by id returns full record, 404 on nonexistent GET/DELETE, DELETE works and is reflected). Frontend: login → More tab tile 'AI Doc Summarizer' visible and routes to /tools/doc-summarizer. All testIDs present (ds-pick, ds-focus-{7 focuses}, ds-save-toggle, ds-summarize). File picker accepts a TXT via expo-document-picker (file chooser dialog), filename rendered, financial pill activates. Summarize call returned ds-result panel in ~12s with real Claude, history row appeared, ds-hist-open-{id} renders the saved summary, ds-hist-delete-{id} after confirm() removed the row. Zero console errors during full flow.
critical_code_review_comments: doc_summarizer.py is clean (~330 lines), uses async Mongo, strips _id and user_id from responses, list view omits extracted_text/source_b64, file size cap 12MB enforced before LLM call, focus validated against FOCUS_PRESETS, save flag normalized via str().lower().
Minor: empty-file check happens after read() so a 13MB body is fully buffered before the size check rejects it — acceptable for current scale but could be moved to a streaming guard for hardening.
Frontend tools/doc-summarizer.tsx is well-structured (~480 lines), uses StyleSheet, all interactive elements have testIDs, web confirm dialog used for delete, history refreshed after save.
seed_data_creation: Test creates and then deletes its own saved-summary row; one no-save summarize call also lands (no persistence). Net zero new persistent rows.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend route mounted via make_doc_summarizer_router in server.py (line ~6092). Real Claude Sonnet 4.5 is used; if LLM budget is later exhausted, tests gracefully pytest.skip on 503 — that contract is intentional and not a bug. Frontend login testID is login-submit-button (not login-submit).
